title: Indie macOS app launch task overrides

FND-02.action: Identify competing Mac apps and indirect alternatives, including browser tools, command-line workflows, manual processes, and doing nothing.
FND-02.done_when: A comparison covers Mac App Store and direct-download competitors, supported macOS versions, architecture support, price, strengths, weaknesses, and positioning.

FND-03.action: Choose three core discovery terms and inspect the live Mac App Store and web results on a Mac.
FND-03.rationale: Early discovery research informs positioning and creates a baseline across the channels where Mac customers actually search.
FND-03.done_when: Three terms are documented with search intent, current Mac competitors, relevant discovery channels, and why the app can compete.

FND-04.action: Select the macOS business model, distribution channels, and initial price.
FND-04.rationale: Distribution changes commerce, licensing, support, and update obligations, so decide the channel and pricing hypothesis together.
FND-04.done_when: The Mac App Store, direct, or dual-channel decision is recorded with price points, trial or free tier, licensing approach, taxes and refunds owner, and a reasoned hypothesis.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: confirm the Paid Apps Agreement when charging, App Store price points, and StoreKit rules for in-app purchases or subscriptions.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: choose a payment processor, license or account recovery flow, refund policy, tax handling, and what happens when the licensing service is unavailable.

FND-05.action: Create the distribution records and signing ownership needed for the selected macOS channels.
FND-05.rationale: Early channel setup exposes identity, contract, certificate, and access blockers before they threaten the release date.
FND-05.done_when: Bundle identifiers, signing team, certificate owner and backup, versioning scheme, distribution channels, and release owner are documented and accessible.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: create the macOS app record in App Store Connect with the correct bundle ID, SKU, primary language, category, and availability.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: create Developer ID Application and, when needed, Developer ID Installer certificates; document who can sign, notarize, and rotate them.

FND-06.action: Publish a minimal Mac-focused landing page with the value proposition, real product screenshot, system requirements, email signup, and the selected acquisition action.
FND-06.rationale: A public destination can collect interest, explain compatibility, and route customers to the correct channel before release.
FND-06.done_when: The HTTPS page works on desktop and mobile, states minimum macOS and hardware support, and its signup, pre-order, purchase, or download action is tested.

STB-01.action: Draft the macOS listing and product-page metadata as one system: name, subtitle or tagline, keywords, category, compatibility, and release notes.
STB-01.rationale: Consistent channel copy must sell the product while setting accurate expectations about compatibility, pricing, permissions, and delivery.
STB-01.done_when: Copy reflects Mac workflows, accurately states system and architecture support, meets the selected channels' limits, and avoids duplicate or prohibited terms.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: complete the localized name, subtitle, keyword field, category, copyright, and platform version information in App Store Connect.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: prepare the website title and description, download filename, version notes, system requirements, license terms, and signed artifact metadata.

STB-02.action: Create macOS screenshots that show real desktop workflows, window chrome, menus, and meaningful app states without exposing private data.
STB-02.rationale: Desktop screenshots are a primary conversion surface and must prove that the app fits real Mac workflows at a readable scale.
STB-02.done_when: A coherent screenshot set covers the primary workflows, remains legible at listing size, and matches every selected channel's required dimensions.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: upload 1-10 screenshots in an accepted 16:10 size: 1280x800, 1440x900, 2560x1600, or 2880x1800 pixels.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: publish optimized 16:10 product images and, when useful, a short demo that shows installation and the first successful workflow.

STB-03.action: Write macOS sales copy that leads with the desktop outcome, then explains workflows, integrations, compatibility, privacy, and the purchase model.
STB-03.done_when: The copy is specific, scannable, accurate about permissions and limitations, localized as needed, and consistent across selected channels.

STB-04.action: Prepare channel-specific editorial and discovery outreach with the launch story, differentiated Mac experience, timing, and supporting assets.
STB-04.rationale: Store editors, reviewers, directories, and newsletters need lead time and channel-appropriate evidence to evaluate a Mac app.
STB-04.done_when: The relevant nomination or outreach is submitted with the release date, supported markets, product story, screenshots, and working links.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: submit a Featuring Nomination in App Store Connect at least three weeks before launch when editorial consideration is relevant.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: pitch appropriate Mac app directories, newsletters, reviewers, and communities; App Store featuring is not applicable to this channel.

STB-05.action: Complete macOS compliance, entitlement, signing, packaging, and reviewer or installer blockers for every selected channel.
STB-05.rationale: Sandbox, entitlement, signature, notarization, privacy, and reviewer failures can all block installation or release even when the app works in development.
STB-05.done_when: Privacy disclosures and purpose strings match behavior, required entitlements are minimal, nested code is signed correctly, clean-machine installation succeeds, and each channel's review or trust checks pass.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: enable App Sandbox, request only required entitlements, complete app privacy, age rating, export compliance, support URL, review notes, and non-expiring demo access.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: enable Hardened Runtime, sign the app and all nested executables with Developer ID, use a secure timestamp, remove get-task-allow, and prepare the final ZIP, DMG, or PKG for notarization.

STB-06.action: Start external macOS beta testing with focused coverage for minimum and current macOS versions, Apple silicon and Intel when supported, permissions, upgrades, keyboard use, windows, menus, and sleep or wake.
STB-06.rationale: A real Mac beta matrix reveals architecture, permission, lifecycle, installation, and update failures that a developer machine can hide.
STB-06.done_when: External testers can install an authentic build on the supported matrix, complete the core workflow, submit feedback, and receive a beta update.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: distribute the beta with TestFlight for Mac and provide precise What to Test guidance.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: distribute a Developer ID-signed and notarized beta through a private download or beta update channel; do not train testers to bypass Gatekeeper.

STB-07.action: Prepare an approvable or notarized macOS release candidate and stage the selected release channels without making the app public.
STB-07.rationale: Validating each channel early creates margin for App Review questions, signing defects, notarization failures, and broken install or update paths.
STB-07.done_when: Every selected channel has an installable release candidate, verified release controls, reproducible install and update tests, and documented blockers.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: upload the build, submit it for App Review, configure manual release, and publish a pre-order when eligible.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: archive with Developer ID, submit with notarytool, inspect the notarization log, staple the ticket, verify with Gatekeeper, and stage the signed download plus update feed.

STB-08.action: Share the trackable pre-order, waitlist, beta, or early-access link across owned channels.
STB-08.rationale: A channel-correct early action turns interest into measurable demand without promising an availability state that does not exist.
STB-08.done_when: Every owned channel uses the correct tracked link, the destination works on a clean Mac and in target regions, and the copy names the actual availability state.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: use the approved pre-order link when a pre-order is live.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: use a waitlist, paid early access, or signed beta link; do not describe it as an App Store pre-order.

STB-10.action: Assemble a Mac press kit with fact sheet, full-resolution screenshots, app icon, system requirements, privacy and pricing summary, contact details, links, and concise story angles.
STB-10.done_when: One public link exposes current assets and accurately describes the selected distribution and universal-purchase availability without requiring access permission.

LWP-02.action: Enable release-channel, credential, crash, support, commerce, and delivery notifications needed to detect a blocked or unhealthy macOS launch.
LWP-02.rationale: Fast awareness prevents review, certificate, notarization, download, payment, or update failures from sitting unnoticed.
LWP-02.done_when: The correct owners receive and test relevant alerts, credential expiry is tracked, and release status can be checked without relying on one person's machine.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: enable App Store Connect notifications for review and status changes.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: monitor signing certificate expiry, notarization failures, download availability, update-feed publication, payment webhooks, and license-service health.

LWP-04.action: Finalize the macOS release candidate with a full week of margin, then test fresh install, upgrade, relaunch, and removal on clean supported Macs.
LWP-04.rationale: A seven-day buffer absorbs App Review delays, signing or notarization corrections, and failures found only in clean install or upgrade tests.
LWP-04.done_when: The exact release artifact passes the architecture and OS matrix, channel trust or review checks, permissions, core workflows, update path, and rollback plan.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: submit the final reviewed build and monitor App Review status.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: notarize and staple the exact final artifact, verify signatures recursively, record its checksum, and test it after downloading over HTTPS.

LWP-06.action: Freeze noncritical changes and identify the exact macOS artifacts, checksums, store version, download URL, and update-feed state that will ship.
LWP-06.done_when: Only release blockers may change, each channel's immutable release candidate is identified, and owners know the launch and rollback runbook.

DAY-01.action: Release the verified macOS app through every selected channel and perform a purchase, download, first-launch, and update smoke test as a customer.
DAY-01.rationale: Release execution must use the exact reviewed or notarized artifacts and channel controls already verified before launch day.
DAY-01.done_when: The intended version installs without security bypasses, launches on the supported matrix, completes its core workflow, and reports the expected version and channel.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: release the approved app or verify that the pre-order transitions to available in target storefronts.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: publish the notarized artifact and checksum, activate the stable update feed, and verify Gatekeeper acceptance from a fresh browser download.

DAY-02.action: Replace signup, waitlist, beta, or pre-order calls to action with the correct live macOS purchase or download actions.
DAY-02.done_when: The production website points to each live channel, states compatibility and pricing accurately, and tracking survives redirects to the store or payment provider.

FBF-01.action: Centralize public reviews, support mail, purchase issues, crashes, installation failures, update failures, beta feedback, and analytics in one log.
FBF-01.done_when: Each report includes channel, macOS version, hardware architecture, app version, installation source, severity, owner, status, and evidence.

FBF-03.action: Fix the highest-severity macOS defect and send a tested update through every affected release channel.
FBF-03.rationale: Fast, focused repair protects early trust, but every corrected Mac artifact must still pass its channel's security and release controls.
FBF-03.done_when: The defect is reproduced on the affected OS and architecture, fixed, regression-tested, passed through each channel's release controls, and verified through the real update path.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: request expedited review only for a genuine critical case and include reproducible steps for the public defect.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: notarize and staple the corrected artifact, publish an authenticated update, and verify both in-app upgrade and fresh download.

FBF-04.action: Reply respectfully and specifically to public reviews, directory comments, and customer support requests.
FBF-04.done_when: Public reviews and support cases have a useful response or explicit priority reason, channel differences are handled accurately, and no reply exposes private information.

FBF-05.action: Ship macOS version 1.0.1 only when a tested fix or useful improvement earns the update.
FBF-05.done_when: The update passes the supported OS and architecture matrix, fresh-install and upgrade tests, each channel's release validation, staged rollout, and release monitoring.

FBF-06.action: Ask for an App Store rating, public review, or testimonial at a natural moment of user success after the first update settles.
FBF-06.done_when: The request matches the installation channel, avoids onboarding and interruption, does not gate functionality, and has version and engagement safeguards.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: use Apple's system review prompt rather than a custom rating prompt.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: request a testimonial or review for an appropriate public directory; never imitate the App Store rating sheet.

FBF-07.action: Record discovery baselines for the three core terms across the selected store, web, and directory surfaces.
FBF-07.done_when: Rank or visibility, channel, storefront or region, competitors, conversion signal, and measurement date are stored for each term.

LTL-02.action: Revisit each selected macOS product page using real discovery, conversion, review, refund, activation, update, and retention data.
LTL-02.rationale: Channel-page decisions become better grounded after the app has real discovery, purchase, activation, and retention outcomes.
LTL-02.done_when: Proposed copy, creative, pricing, compatibility, or onboarding changes cite channel-specific evidence and define the next comparison window.

LTL-03.action: Decide whether paid acquisition is economically viable for each macOS distribution channel before spending.
LTL-03.done_when: Channel-specific conversion, commission and payment costs, refunds, support cost, net lifetime value, and maximum acquisition cost are documented with test guardrails.

distribution_guidance.app-store: Mac App Store: evaluate Apple Ads only where inventory and targeting support the intended app and storefronts.
distribution_guidance.direct: Direct: evaluate search, sponsorship, directory, newsletter, affiliate, or retargeting channels against completed purchases and activated licenses.

LTL-05.action: Review macOS discovery, paid acquisition, crash-free use, refund, activation, and update-adoption results, then roll channel-specific lessons into the next release.
LTL-05.done_when: Ineffective work is stopped, winning terms and channels are recorded, unresolved compatibility or update risks have owners, and the next experiment is defined.
